Article.ArticleTitle: mRNA vaccine against SARS-CoV-2 response is comparable between patients on dialysis and healthy controls after adjustment for age, gender and history of COVID-19 infection.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Department of Nephrology and Dialysis, Ramsay Santé, Hôpital Privé Claude Galien, Quincy-sous-Sénart, France. rostotom@orange.fr.
Collège de Médecine des Hôpitaux de Paris, Paris, France. rostotom@orange.fr.
Department of Nephrology and Dialysis, Ramsay Santé, Hôpital Privé Claude Galien, Quincy-sous-Sénart, France.
Infection Prevention and Control Registered Nurse, Ramsay Santé, Hôpital Privé Claude Galien, Quincy-sous-Sénart, France.
Department of Nephrology and Dialysis, Ramsay Santé, Hôpital Privé Claude Galien, Quincy-sous-Sénart, France.
Department of Nephrology and Dialysis, Ramsay Santé, Hôpital Privé Claude Galien, Quincy-sous-Sénart, France.
Department of Nephrology and Dialysis, Ramsay Santé, Hôpital Privé Claude Galien, Quincy-sous-Sénart, France.

Grant.GrantID: This research was funded by Ramsay Health Cooperation Group for Education
No. COS-RGDS-2021-06-075). Ramsay Santé supported this study for Clinical samples analysis (with support from the Nexelis/Q2 Solutions corporation)
proofreading in English (with support from the Newmed Publishing corporation)
statistical analysis (with support from the StatEthic corporation).

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Conflict of interest: GR reports receiving consultancy fees from Astellas (board on Roxadustat, 2019–2021, 2023), GlaxoSmithKline (he serves on the scientific board for Daprodustat in 2022 and 2023), Vifor (board on Difelikefalin, 2021–2023). He reports research funding for scientific presentations from Amgen, Astellas, Baxter, Hemotech, Gambro Hospal, Nipro, Physidia, and Theradial. He also reports receiving honoraria from Amgen, Astellas, GlaxoSmithKline, Roche, Sanofi, and Vifor; and is on the speakers’ bureau for Astellas and Baxter. GR is employed as a nephrologist by the Société d’Exercice Libéral à Responsabilité Limitée (SELARL) (a limited liability company for non-commercial services) de Néphrologie et de Dialyse des Drs Rostoker et Associés, and he is the executive managing officer of the SELARL. SR is a statistician and is employed by the company StatEthic in Levallois-Perret, which she founded. MM is employed as a nurse coordinator in the dialysis center by the private hospital Claude Galien in Quincy-sous-Sénart, HC is employed as an Infection Prevention and Control Registered Nurse by the Claude Galien private hospital in Quincy-sous-Sénart. GB is a collaborator of the SELARL de Néphrologie et de Dialyse des Drs Rostoker et Associés at the Claude Galien private hospital in Quincy-sous-Sénart. MG and CL are employed by the SELARL de Néphrologie et de Dialyse des Drs Rostoker et Associés at the Claude Galien private hospital in Quincy-sous-Sénart. LG is a PhD in Microbiology/Immunology, he is the Global Head of Vaccine Sciences at IQVIA Laboratories in Laval, Canada. The funders had no role in the design of the study; in the collection, analyses, or interpretation of data; in the writing of the manuscript; nor in the decision to publish the results. Ethical approval: This retrospective observational study was conducted in accordance with French law and did not require approval by an ethics committee. It was conducted in accordance with the Declaration of Helsinki. The study protocol follows the MR-004 reference methodology defined by the Commission Nationale de l’Informatique et des Libertés (CNIL), the French authority for data privacy protection. This study received approval from the scientific committee of the Ramsay Health Cooperation Group for Education and Research (GCS Ramsay Santé, No. COS-RGDS-2021-06-075) and was registered on the public health data hub (registration number F20220207122956). Informed consent to participate: All participants received information about the study and gave informed oral consent. All data were analyzed unless participants refused to take part in the study.

Reference.Citation: El Karoui K, De Vriese AS (2022) COVID-19 in dialysis: clinical impact, immune response, prevention, and treatment. Kidney Int 101:883–894. 10.1016/j.kint.2022.01.022
Barouch DH (2022) Covid-19 vaccines: immunity, variants, boosters. N Engl J Med 387:1011–1020. 10.1056/NEJMra2206573
Ashby DR, Caplin B, Corbett RW et al (2022) Severity of COVID-19 after vaccination among hemodialysis patients: an observational cohort study. Clin J Am Soc Nephrol 17:843–850. 10.2215/CJN.16621221
Mink S, Fraunberger P (2023) Anti-SARS-CoV-2 antibody testing: role and indications. J Clin Med 12:7575. 10.3390/jcm12247575

Benedikt S, Rubey H, Treipl A et al (2021) Haemodialysis patients show a highly diminished antibody response after COVID-19 mRNA vaccination compared with healthy controls. Nephrol Dial Transplant 36:1709–1716. 10.1093/ndt/gfab179

Espi M, Charmetant X, Barba T et al (2021) The ROMANOV study found impaired humoral and cellular immune responses to SARS-CoV-2 mRNA vaccine in virus-unexposed patients receiving maintenance hemodialysis. Kidney Int 100:928–936. 10.1016/j.kint.2021.07.005

Bewley KR, Coombes NS, Gagnon L et al (2021) Quantification of SARS-CoV-2 neutralizing antibody by wild-type plaque reduction neutralization, microneutralization and pseudotyped virus neutralization assays. Nat Protoc 16:3114–3140. 10.1038/s41596-021-00536-y

Knell AI, Böhm AK, Jäger M et al (2023) Virus-subtype-specific cellular and humoral immune response to a COVID-19 mRNA vaccine in chronic kidney disease patients and renal transplant recipients. Microorganisms 11:1756. 10.3390/microorganisms11071756
Cohen J (1988) Statistical power analysis for the behavioral sciences, 2nd edn. Lawrence Erlbaum Associates, Hillsdale, NJ
